title: Regression hardening — flaky-test audit + stable selector sweep
predecessor: M35-T8 shipped 2026-04-20 (commit a8dc98e — 'chore(m35): M35 T8 — v1.0.0 → v1.1.0 + Phase 5 badge pin'). Bumped version field 1.0.0 → 1.1.0 across 7 workspace package.json files (root + apps/desktop + 5 packages/*, matching a repo-wide find sweep — plan doc's literal 'all 6' was a plan-authoring drift). Added NEW apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/app/top-bar.test.tsx (+2 unit tests → 1164) via source-string audit style per M35 T3 convention (no jsdom, no React rendering — vitest env is 'node'). Test 1 matches <Badge>…</Badge> regex in top-bar.tsx and asserts trimmed children === 'Phase 5' (canary against accidental Phase 6 flip). Test 2 JSON.parse of apps/desktop/package.json asserts version === '1.1.0' (semver pin). Path correction: top-bar.tsx lives at apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/app/ (NOT the plan-speculative .../components/app/). Grep audit clean — no stale 1.0.0 claims outside CHANGELOG.md and release.yml's example 'v1.0.0' comment. Predecessors T0 (da4ce1f) → T1 (b68d09b) → T2 (1108247) → T3 (51393f8) → T4 (0f8b51c) → T5 (3cffe29) → T6 (a966deb) → T7 (cb0ab4c) → T8 (a8dc98e). Unit baseline now 1164. E2E baseline 11 spec files / 12 Playwright cases (from T2).
goal: Two small regression guards that harden the test suite against flake and against authors forgetting to use the data-* stable-selector pattern the M30/M31/M32/M34 specs established. (a) Regex-scan every apps/desktop/e2e/*.spec.ts and flag any `page.waitForTimeout(N)` or equivalent `setTimeout`-for-sync patterns where N > 100 ms — prefer `expect.poll` / `toPass` / `waitFor` instead. (b) Regex-scan every spec and assert at least one `data-*` attribute locator is used somewhere in the file (per the M30 data-step-kind, M33 data-copilot-insight-id, M34 data-copilot-toolbar-toggle convention). (c) Optional — clear the M30 T2 pre-existing lint error at packages/intelligence/src/nlu/entity-resolver.ts:352 if it is still dormant (orchestrator.json baseline.lintErrorsNote references it; current.lintErrors is already 0, so this is belt-and-suspenders). Tests delta: +2 unit (1164 → 1166). Zero spec-behavior changes unless the scan flags a real offender — in which case fix the offending spec in the same commit.
acceptance: NEW vitest spec scanning e2e/*.spec.ts files. Placement discipline: the per-workspace apps/desktop/vitest.config.ts EXCLUDES e2e/** — a regression-guards test must live under src/ so vitest picks it up. Recommended path: apps/desktop/src/e2e-regression-guards.test.ts. Recon the config before authoring; do not invent a sibling directory unless vitest config is extended in lockstep.

Test #1 (flake audit): use readdirSync to walk apps/desktop/e2e/*.spec.ts; for each file, regex for `\.waitForTimeout\(\s*(\d+)` and any `setTimeout\s*\(` used as a synchronization primitive. Assert zero matches where the numeric argument exceeds 100 ms (the 100ms floor allows drag-lag quirks without masking real polls). If matches exist, fix the offending spec in the SAME commit by swapping to `expect.poll` / `toPass` / `locator.waitFor` and re-run E2E to green before continuing.

Test #2 (stable-selector sweep): for each apps/desktop/e2e/*.spec.ts, regex-assert at least one `data-[a-z][a-z0-9-]*` attribute selector occurrence (e.g. `[data-copilot-insight-id]`, `[data-step-kind=...]`, `[data-copilot-toolbar-toggle]`). Text-content fallbacks (`getByText`, `getByRole({ name: ... })`) are fine as secondary matchers but each spec must include at least one data-* to anchor the stable-selector contract.

Optional M30 T2 lint clearance: packages/intelligence/src/nlu/entity-resolver.ts:352 — if `pnpm lint` still surfaces the pre-existing error, fix in-line. Current `orchestrator.json.current.lintErrors: 0` suggests M35 T1 already neutralized it, so skip cleanly if dormant — only touch the file if the lint run genuinely flags it.

Tests green after addition: full `pnpm test` (1164 + 2 = 1166) and Electron rebuild + Playwright (11 spec files / 12 cases) stay green. If Playwright surfaces any flake during the T9 pass, capture the flake shape + fix in the same T9 commit — T9's explicit purpose is hardening against flake.
Atomic commit: `test(m35): M35 T9 — flaky-test audit + stable selector sweep`.

Ledger commit: `chore(loki): M35 T9 — commit ledger (<sha>)` — roll orchestrator.json tasksCompleted 9 → 10 + inflight {taskId: M35-T10, lastShipped: M35-T9, lastShippedCommit: <sha>}, update pending.json T9 status pending → shipped with commit + shippedAt + verificationGates, overwrite current-task.json to M35-T10 (final verification + docs reconciliation + milestone marker).
outOfScope: NO version changes (T8 closed that surface).
NO CHANGELOG edits (T7 closed that surface).
NO CLAUDE.md edits (T10 handles the Phase 5 COMPLETE line flip).
NO retrospective / demo / user-guide edits (T4 / T5 / T6 closed those surfaces).
NO sweeping spec rewrites — minimal surgical edits only where a guard catches a real offender.
carryForward: 'DO NOT invent' rule from T7 surpriseNote + T8 carry-forward continues to apply. M28 + M29 still have no [Unreleased] entries and that is intentional — do NOT backfill.

The source-string audit convention (T3 / T8) remains the preferred style for renderer-side unit tests given the node-only vitest environment. If a T9 guard needs browser DOM, don't introduce jsdom — keep it in Playwright.
The M31 data-step-kind + M33 data-copilot-insight-id + M34 data-copilot-toolbar-toggle attribute pattern is the canonical stable-selector surface — T9 asserts the pattern, doesn't extend it.
Atomic work commit + separate ledger commit cadence (CLAUDE.md M35 T7 framing) unchanged.
surpriseWatch: (a) Vitest config: apps/desktop/vitest.config.ts EXCLUDES e2e/**. A regression-guards test that reads e2e/*.spec.ts files from disk must live under src/ to be picked up.
(b) Windows path handling: readdirSync against apps/desktop/e2e/ returns bare filenames without the parent prefix. Build paths explicitly with path.join to avoid platform flake.

(c) False-positive on test fixtures: if any E2E spec stores a 'setTimeout' literal inside a canned-provider fixture string (unlikely but possible), the regex may flag it. Use a multi-line regex that only matches top-level `page.waitForTimeout(` calls, not arbitrary substring matches.
(d) M30 T2 lint row may already be dormant — T1 cleared the error baseline. Don't re-introduce the error by over-editing the file; grep + run `pnpm lint` first.

(e) Playwright flake shape: if any of the 11 spec files go red during the T9 verification run, treat it as a legitimate T9 target (hardening against flake IS the task). Capture the flake in the commit body.
risks: If the regression-guards test is placed under apps/desktop/e2e/ by accident, vitest won't pick it up and the guard silently never runs. Placement discipline is the single biggest T9 risk.

If the waitForTimeout regex catches a legitimate drag-drop settling delay (kanban specs occasionally need ~200ms for React DnD to settle), the mechanical rule conflicts with UX realities. The 100ms floor is the negotiation — above 100ms should have a comment explaining why.
If the data-* assertion breaks a spec that genuinely has none (e.g. a pure text-content test), T9 must add a minimal data-* attribute to the surface under test rather than weaken the assertion.
commitTemplate: test(m35): M35 T9 — flaky-test audit + stable selector sweep
notes: T9 is the last active-development task before T10, which is pure verification + docs reconciliation + milestone marker. After T10, M35 is complete, Phase 5 is complete, and the next session opens Phase 6.
